questions.question: What is the time period considered a long period in the context of metric spaces?
questions.explanation: In compactness, an 8-hour time period is considered long

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT an example of a compact metric space?
questions.explanation: Sqrt(-1) is not a subset of the reals

questions.question: What is the definition of a compact metric space?
options: A space is compact if every open cover has a finite subcover
A space is compact if every closed set is compact
A space is compact if every open set is compact
questions.explanation: A compact metric space is one where every open cover has a finite subcover

questions.question: What is the property of compact metric spaces that allows for the Heine-Borel theorem to be applied?
questions.explanation: The property of compactness allows for the Heine-Borel theorem to be applied

questions.question: Can every metric space be compact?
questions.explanation: No, not every metric space can be compact
